title: Dilute ready to use large volume containers of phenylephrine
abstract: Ready to use large volume containers of dilute solutions of phenylephrine having excellent shelf stability.
claims:
Claims 1) A drug product in a hermetically sealed intravenous ready to use collapsible drip bag, wherein: a) the bag comprises two opposed walls sealed around their peripheries comprising an inner ply of translucent or transparent polypropylene; b) the bag defines an interior volume; c) the interior volume comprises: i) a liquid volume comprising from 75 to 550 ml or from 100 to 350 ml or from 250 to 325 ml of a liquid formulation; ii) a gaseous headspace occupying from 0 to 25% or from 2 to 15% of the interior volume; d) the liquid formulation comprises: i) phenylephrine or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof at a concentration of from 0.001 to 2.0 mg/ml or from 0.01 to 0.8 mg/ml; ii) a tonicity agent; iii) an acidic pH adjusting agent; and iv) water; e) the liquid formulation has a pH of from 2.5 to 6.5, from 2.6 to 5.5, from 2.7 to 5.0, from 2.8 to 4.5, from 3.0 to 6.5, from 3.0 to 5.5, from 3.0 to 5.0, from 3.0 to 4.5, from 3.0 to 3.8, from 3.0 to 3.6, from 3.0 to 3.4, or from 3.0 to 3.2; and f) the liquid formulation excludes sulfites and/or sodium hydroxide. 2) The drug product of claim 1, wherein the liquid formulation further comprises nitrogen. 3) The drug product of claim 1, wherein the liquid formulation has a pH of from 3.0 to 6.5. 4) The drug product of claim 1, wherein the liquid formulation excludes sulfites and sodium hydroxide. 5) The drug product of claim 1, wherein the acidic pH adjusting agent is hydrochloric acid. 6) The drug product of claim 1, wherein the liquid formulation further comprises nitrogen, the liquid formulation has a pH of from 3.0 to 6.5, the liquid formulation excludes sulfites, and the acidic pH adjusting agent is hydrochloric acid. 

7) The drug product of claim 1, wherein the liquid formulation further comprises nitrogen, the liquid formulation has a pH of from 3.0 to 6.5, the liquid formulation excludes sulfites and sodium hydroxide, and the acidic pH adjusting agent is hydrochloric acid. 8) The drug product of claim 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7, comprising phenylephrine hydrochloride at a concentration of 0.08 mg/ml or 0.4 mg/ml based on the weight of the hydrochloride salt. 9) The drug product of claim 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7, wherein the tonicity agent is sodium chloride, preferably in a tonicifying effective amount. 10) The drug product of claim 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7, in the absence of: a) an antioxidant such as a sulfite; or b) a chelating agent such as EDTA; or c) an antimicrobial preservative; or d) a basic pH adjusting agent such as sodium hydroxide; or e) a combination thereof. 11) The drug product of claim 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7, in the absence of: a) sulfites; b) a chelating agent; c) optionally an antimicrobial preservative; and d) optionally a basic pH adjusting agent 12) The drug product of claim 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7, wherein the liquid formulation has a pH of from 3.0 to 3.4 or from 3.0 to 3.2. 13) The drug product of claim 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7, wherein the bag wall has an oxygen transmission rate greater than 0.5, 1.0, 20, 100, 500, 1000, or even 4000 cm 3 Â·20Î¼m/m 2 Â·dayÂ·atm, preferably less than 40,000 or 10,000 cm 3 Â·20Î¼m/m 2 Â·dayÂ·atm, at room temperature and atmospheric pressure, and the liquid formulation comprises no more than 1%, 0.5%, 0.2%, or 0.1% (w/w) oxidative degradants such as phenylephrone. 14) The drug product of claim 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7, wherein the bag wall has an oxygen transmission rate greater than 0.5, 1.0, 20, 100, 500, 1000, or even 4000 cm 3 Â·20Î¼m/m 2 Â·dayÂ·atm, preferably less than 40,000 or 10,000 cm 3 Â·20Î¼m/m 2 Â·dayÂ·atm, at room temperature and atmospheric pressure, the liquid formulation comprises no more than 1%, 0.5%, 0.2%, or 0.1% (w/w) oxidative degradants such as phenylephrine, and the liquid formulation lacks sulfites and chelating agents. 

15) A drug product in a hermetically sealed intravenous ready to use collapsible drip bag, wherein: a) the bag defines an interior volume; b) the interior volume comprises: i) a liquid volume comprising from 75 to 550 ml or from 100 to 350 ml or from 250 to 325 ml of a liquid formulation; ii) a gaseous headspace occupying from 0 to 25% or from 2 to 15% of the interior volume; c) the liquid formulation comprises: i) phenylephrine or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof at a concentration of from 0.001 to 2.0 mg/ml or from 0.01 to 0.8 mg/ml; ii) a tonicity agent; iii) an acidic pH adjusting agent; and iv) water; d) the liquid formulation has a pH of from 2.5 to 6.5, from 2.6 to 5.5, from 2.7 to 5.0, from 2.8 to 4.5, from 3.0 to 6.5, from 3.0 to 5.5, from 3.0 to 5.0, from 3.0 to 4.5, from 3.0 to 3.8, from 3.0 to 3.6, from 3.0 to 3.4, or from 3.0 to 3.2; and e) the liquid formulation excludes sulfites and/or sodium hydroxide. 16) The drug product of claim 15, wherein the liquid formulation further comprises nitrogen. 17) The drug product of claim 15, wherein the liquid formulation excludes sodium hydroxide. 18) The drug product of claim 15, wherein the acidic pH adjusting agent is hydrochloric acid. 19) The drug product of claim 15, wherein the liquid formulation further comprises nitrogen, the liquid formulation excludes sodium hydroxide, and the acidic pH adjusting agent is hydrochloric acid. 20) The drug product of claim 15, wherein the formulation excludes a chelating agent, an antimicrobial preservative, and a basic pH adjusting agent 21) The drug product of claim 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 20, comprising phenylephrine hydrochloride at a concentration of 0.08 mg/ml or 0.4 mg/ml based on the weight of the hydrochloride salt 22) The drug product of claim 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 20, wherein the acidic pH adjusting agent is hydrochloric acid. 

23) The drag product of claim 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 20, wherein the tonicity agent is sodium chloride, preferably in a tonicifying effective amount 24) The drag product of claim 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 20, wherein the acidic pH adjusting agent is hydrochloric acid and the tonicity agent is sodium chloride, preferably in a tonicifying effective amount. 25) The drag product of claim 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 20, in the absence of: a) an antioxidant such as a sulfite; or b) a chelating agent such as EDTA; or c) an antimicrobial preservative; or d) a basic pH adjusting agent such as sodium hydroxide; or e) a combination thereof. 26) The drag product of claim 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 20, in the absence of: a) sulfites; b) a chelating agent; c) optionally an antimicrobial preservative; and d) optionally a basic pH adjusting agent 27) The drag product of claim 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 20, wherein the liquid formulation has a pH of from 3.0 to 3.4 or from 3.0 to 3.2. 28) The drag product of claim 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 20, wherein the collapsible drip bag is translucent or transparent, preferably substantially as depicted in Figure 5. 29) The drag product of claim 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 20, wherein the bag has an oxygen transmission rate greater than 0.5, 1.0, 20, 100, 500, 1000, or even 4000 cm 3 Â·20Î¼m/m 2 Â·dayÂ·atm, preferably less than 40,000 or 10,000 cm 3 Â·20Î¼m/m 2 Â·dayÂ·atm, at room temperature and atmospheric pressure, and the liquid formulation comprises no more than 1%, 0.5%, 0.2%, or 0.1% (w/w) oxidative degradants such as phenylephrone. 30) The drag product of claim 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, or 20, wherein the bag has an oxygen transmission rate greater than 0.5, 1.0, 20, 100, 500, 1000, or even 4000 cm 3 Â·20Î¼m/m 2 Â·dayÂ·atm, preferably less than 40,000 or 10,000 cm 3 Â·20Î¼m/m 2 Â·dayÂ·atm, at room temperature and atmospheric pressure, the liquid formulation comprises no more than 1%, 0.5%, 0.2%, or 0.1% (w/w) oxidative degradants such as phenylephrine, and the formulation lacks sulfites and chelating agents. 

31) A method of making a phenylephrine drug product in an intravenous ready to use collapsible drip bag comprising: a) dissolving phenylephrine or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, a tonicity agent, and an acidic pH adjusting agent in water to produce a phenylephrine liquid formulation at a concentration of from 0.001 to 2.0 mg/ml or from 0.01 to 0.8 mg/ml; b) distributing from 75 to 550 ml or from 100 to 350 ml or from 250 to 325 ml of the phenylephrine liquid formulation and an inert gas into the bag, such that the percentage of the bagâs volume occupied by the headspace is from 0 to 25% or from 2 to 15%; and c) hermetically sealing the bag; wherein: i) the liquid formulation excludes sulfites and/or sodium hydroxide; and/or ii) the liquid formulation has a pH of from 3.0 to 6.5. 32) The method of claim 31, wherein the liquid formulation further comprises nitrogen. 33) The method of claim 31, wherein the liquid formulation excludes sulfites and sodium hydroxide. 34) The method of claim 31, wherein the acidic pH adjusting agent is hydrochloric acid. 35) The method of claim 31, wherein the liquid formulation further comprises nitrogen, the liquid formulation excludes sulfites, and the acidic pH adjusting agent is hydrochloric acid. 36) The method of claim 31, wherein the liquid formulation further comprises nitrogen, the liquid formulation excludes sulfites and sodium hydroxide, and the acidic pH adjusting agent is hydrochloric acid. 37) The method of claim 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, or 36, comprising phenylephrine hydrochloride at a concentration of 0.08 mg/ml or 0.4 mg/ml based on the weight of the hydrochloride salt. 38) The method of claim 31 , 32, 33, 34, 35, or 36, wherein the tonicity agent is sodium chloride, preferably in a tonicifying effective amount 39) The method of claim 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, or 36, wherein the formulation excludes: a) an antioxidant such as a sulfite; or b) a chelating agent such as EDTA; or c) an antimicrobial preservative; or 

 d) a basic pH adjusting agent such as sodium hydroxide; or e) a combination thereof. 40) The method of claim 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, or 36, wherein the formulation excludes: a) sulfites; b) a chelating agent; c) optionally an antimicrobial preservative; and d) optionally a basic pH adjusting agent 41) The method of claim 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, or 36, wherein the liquid formulation has a pH of from 3.0 to 3.4 or from 3.0 to 3.2. 42) The method of claim 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, or 36, wherein the collapsible drip bag is translucent or transparent 43) The method of claim 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, or 36, wherein the bag comprises two opposed walls sealed around their peripheries, wherein the walls comprise an interior ply of translucent or transparent polypropylene. 44) The method of claim 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, or 36, further comprising terminal sterilizing the drug product at a temperature in excess of 80, 90, 100 or 121Â°C. 45) A drug product manufactured by the method of any of claims 31-44.
